name: Engineering Manager
description: Memory structure for engineering managers. Covers your role and reports, review preferences, team priorities, and dashboards you track.

memories.name: Manager Role
memories.description: Your management scope, direct reports, and leadership responsibilities
content_prompts: What is your name, title, and which team(s) do you manage?
Who are your direct reports (names, roles, and seniority levels)?
What is your management style and how do you run 1:1s?
What organizational layer are you in (director reports to VP, etc.) and who are your peer managers?
memories.example_content:
---
type: user
name: Manager Role
description: Management scope, direct reports, and leadership context
---

- Name: Sarah Kim, Engineering Manager — Payments Team (8 engineers)
- Reports: Alex (Sr BE), Maria (Sr FE), Dev (SRE), 3 mid-level, 2 junior
- Style: servant leadership, weekly 1:1s (30min), skip-levels monthly
- Reports to VP Eng (James), peers with Platform EM (Carlos) and Growth EM (Priya)

memories.name: Review Preferences
memories.description: How you evaluate code, proposals, and documentation when reviewing
content_prompts: What do you prioritize when reviewing technical proposals (feasibility, timeline, risk)?
What level of detail do you expect in design docs and ADRs?
How do you prefer status updates and progress reports to be structured?
What writing tone do you use for team communications vs. stakeholder updates?
memories.example_content:
---
type: feedback
name: Review Preferences
description: How I evaluate technical work, proposals, and team communications
---

- Proposals: focus on risk assessment and rollback plan first, then timeline
- Design docs: must include alternatives considered and decision rationale
- Status updates: bullet points, lead with blockers, include metrics where possible
- Stakeholder comms: concise, no jargon, always include business impact

memories.name: Team Priorities
memories.description: Current quarter objectives, hiring plans, and active initiatives across your team
content_prompts: What are your team's OKRs or quarterly goals?
Are there any hiring or team composition changes planned?
What are the biggest risks or blockers your team is facing?
What cross-team dependencies or initiatives are you coordinating?
memories.example_content:
---
type: project
name: Team Priorities
description: Quarterly objectives, staffing plans, and active cross-team initiatives
---

- Q1 OKRs: reduce payment failures, launch subscription billing, improve on-call
- Hiring: backfill for departing SRE, open headcount for staff engineer
- Risks: PCI audit timeline tight, dependency on Platform team for infra migration
- Cross-team: coordinating with Growth on checkout redesign, shared API contract

memories.name: Dashboards and Metrics
memories.description: Key metrics you track, dashboards you check, and reporting tools you use
content_prompts: What are the top 3-5 metrics you track for your team's health and performance?
What dashboards do you check daily or weekly?
What tools do you use for project tracking (Jira, Linear, Shortcut)?
Where do you report team metrics to leadership?
memories.example_content:
---
type: reference
name: Dashboards and Metrics
description: Key metrics, dashboards, and reporting tools for team health monitoring
---

- Key metrics: deployment frequency, MTTR, payment failure rate, sprint velocity
- Daily: Grafana payment-health, PagerDuty on-call overview
- Weekly: Linear sprint board, GitHub PR cycle time report
- Leadership reporting: monthly eng metrics deck in Google Slides
